project.name: Stock by Curve
project.description: Capture Finviz stock curve screenshots from a ticker list and save them into a single local PDF file.

version.change: Default paths now auto-resolve from the folder where tk_app.py is located.
Default PDF output folder changed to stock_image_pdf.
PDF filename field now displays full path and filename.
Added a scrollable progress message panel near the progress bar.
Progress panel keeps only the latest 200 lines to stay readable.
When saving PDF, existing target file is deleted first to prevent duplicate confusion.
If Save As is cancelled, temporary PDF is deleted automatically.
Increased default Tk window height so bottom progress messages are visible at startup.

features.feature: Read ticker symbols from a text file, one ticker per line.
Capture selected tickers from Finviz sequentially.
Save captured ticker charts into one multi-page PDF in a user-selected folder.
Support multiple Finviz time intervals through the r query parameter.
Provide a Tkinter desktop UI with ticker checkboxes, Select All, progress, and logs.
Use a 300 DPI capture scale with a default 1280 x 1500 CSS pixel viewport.

files.file: Core Playwright capture logic and command-line batch mode.
Tkinter desktop UI for selecting ticker files, output folders, time intervals, and checked tickers.
Streamlit UI prototype retained for reference.
Default ticker list used by command-line mode and the UI.

requirements.requirement: streamlit, only if app.py is used

notes.note: Select a ticker txt file or use the default stock.txt.
Select an output folder or use the default stock_image_pdf folder.
Check the tickers to capture, then click Start Capture.
Reads stock.txt from the project folder.
Saves images into stock_images by default.
Uses 1 Year, r=y1, unless --range is provided.
